practice_boundaries.detail: Explicitly prohibits turning evidence ranking into clinical advice or treatment recommendation

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 evidence analysis only

functional_suitability.note: Frontmatter contains non-standard fields (category, tags, version) beyond the required name/description — structural inconsistency with the skill format standard

reliability.note: Proceeds with available materials when input is incomplete; no structured minimum-input clarification protocol

performance_context.note: 249 lines with five reference modules; well-proportioned

maintainability.note: Non-standard frontmatter fields (category/subcategory/tags/version) reduce schema compliance and may cause parsing inconsistencies

agent_specific.note: Five citation-role categories provide good citation-use differentiation; description brief and under-specified for triggering

inputs.label: Rank a meta-analysis, RCT, cohort, and mechanism paper on the same question

assertions.text: Evidence family, methodological quality, validation depth, and claim discipline assessed separately
Meta-analysis not automatically ranked #1 without checking execution quality
Citation role assigned to each paper (anchor/high-value/context/mechanistic/caution)
Ranking reasoning explicit — not just an order list
Ranking uncertainties and caveats section present

inputs.label: Rank mixed papers serving different evidence roles — not directly comparable

assertions.text: Papers serving different roles identified as non-comparable rather than forced into single ladder
Different evidence roles explained with comparison logic
Clinical evidence separated from mechanistic evidence for citation purpose
Journal prestige not used as ranking criterion

inputs.label: Rank papers where higher-tier design has weak execution

assertions.text: Poorly executed RCT ranked below well-executed cohort with explicit justification
Execution quality dimensions assessed (sampling, bias control, sample size, statistics)
Overclaim pattern identified in higher-tier but lower-quality paper
Caution citation role correctly applied to overclaiming paper
Statistical significance not equated with methodological reliability
assertions.note: One instance where a statistically significant result was used as partial evidence of methodological strength without acknowledging the distinctness of these two dimensions

key_strengths: Four-dimension ranking framework (evidence family, methodological quality, validation depth, claim discipline) prevents design-label-to-rank conflation
Five citation roles give manuscript authors actionable guidance beyond a generic quality score
Seventeen hard rules explicitly address the most common evidence appraisal errors including prestige ranking and statistical significance conflation
Design-role separation rule prevents forcing non-comparable papers into a misleading single ranking ladder

recommendations.title: Non-standard frontmatter fields violate SKILL.md schema
recommendations.problem: The frontmatter includes non-standard fields: category, subcategory, tags, and version. These fields are not part of the standard skill schema and may cause parser errors or inconsistent behavior in skill loading systems.
recommendations.root_cause: Frontmatter was extended with metadata fields beyond the required name/description/license/skill-author standard.
recommendations.fix: Remove category, subcategory, tags, and version from frontmatter. Move any useful metadata to the first section of the SKILL.md body or a references/metadata.md file.

recommendations.title: Statistical significance used as partial proxy for methodological quality
recommendations.problem: In one evaluation instance, a p < 0.05 result was briefly cited as partial evidence of methodological strength, conflating statistical significance with methodological reliability.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard Rule 3 ('Never treat statistical significance as equivalent to methodological reliability') is present but not reinforced in the execution steps where methodological quality is assessed.
recommendations.fix: Add an explicit check in Step 3 (Methodological Quality): 'Do not use statistical significance or effect size magnitude as evidence of good methods — assess the sampling, bias control, and study design directly.'

recommendations.title: Description too brief to differentiate from generic citation management or literature review tools
recommendations.problem: The description is a single sentence that does not include key trigger phrases that would distinguish this skill from general literature tools.
recommendations.fix: Add trigger phrases: 'citation priority', 'anchor citation vs background citation', 'methodological quality tier', 'validation depth ranking', 'compare evidence families'.
